claims.safe_public_wording: Five new local held-out operational tasks were selected outside the Proofs 018-020 task titles.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Overstating this would imply broader novelty than the recorded title and domain separation supports.

claims.safe_public_wording: The scorer evaluated solution_A, solution_B, and solution_C labels before reveal.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Overstating this would hide any accidental source-identifying text in blind artifacts.

claims.safe_public_wording: The blind scoring artifact records no access to production mode names during scoring.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Overstating this would weaken the leakage control the proof is meant to test.

claims.safe_public_wording: The hidden mapping records reveal after the blind score lock and binds to the blind score hash.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Overstating this would permit self-confirming score edits after reveal.

claims.safe_public_wording: MCP-seasoned refs are absent from blind scoring artifacts.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Overstating this would conceal evaluator leakage.

claims.safe_public_wording: After reveal, the MCP-seasoned condition was first on all five local tasks.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Overstating this would turn a local blind-scored result into an unsupported general result.

claims.safe_public_wording: After reveal, the MCP-seasoned aggregate score exceeded both controls in this proof.
claims.risk_if_overstated: Overstating this would imply superiority outside the recorded task set.

claims.safe_public_wording: The final verdict is bounded to the five local held-out tasks and this rubric.
claims.risk_if_overstated: If overstated, the evidence boundary would be exceeded.
